name: K-Means
description: K-Means is an unsupervised clustering algorithm that groups data points into a predefined number of clusters by minimizing intra-cluster distance.
input: It takes in a DataFrame as input and performs K-Means clustering
output: The input DataFrame is passed along to the next Processors

fields.name: modelIdentifier
fields.title: Model Identifier
fields.description: Optional identifier used when running the model inside loops. Format: $loop_columnName1_columnName2.

fields.name: featuresCol
fields.title: Features Column
fields.description: Vector column containing numerical features used for clustering.

fields.name: k
fields.title: K
fields.description: Number of clusters to divide the dataset into.

fields.name: maxIter
fields.title: Max Iterations
fields.description: Maximum number of iterations the algorithm will run before stopping.

fields.name: predictionCol
fields.title: Prediction Column
fields.description: Column containing the assigned cluster index for each record.

fields.name: seed
fields.title: Seed
fields.description: Random seed used to initialize cluster centers for reproducible results.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerence
fields.description: Convergence threshold used to determine when the algorithm should stop iterating.

fields.name: initMode
fields.title: initMode
fields.description: Initialization method used to select initial cluster centers. k-means|| provides faster and more stable convergence.

fields.name: initSteps
fields.title: initSteps
fields.description: Number of steps used in the k-means|| initialization process. Ignored for other initialization modes.

fields.name: distanceMeasure
fields.title: distanceMeasure
fields.description: Distance metric used to measure similarity between data points and cluster centers.

fields.name: weightCol
fields.title: Weight Column
fields.description: Optional column specifying the relative importance of each record during clustering.
